← All posts February 26, 2026

The pigeonhole proof hiding in every list of numbers

  • math
  • combinatorics
  • pigeonhole

This one made the rounds after Dunkin’ put a math challenge on during the 2025 Super Bowl, but the underlying theorem is a combinatorics staple:

Given any nnn natural numbers a1,a2,…,ana_1, a_2, \dots, a_na1​,a2​,…,an​, there exist indices 0≤j<k≤n0 \le j < k \le n0≤j<k≤n such that n∣∑i=j+1kai.n \mid \sum_{i=j+1}^{k} a_i.n∣∑i=j+1k​ai​.

In words: some consecutive block of the list sums to a multiple of nnn. Not just some subset — a contiguous run.

Prefix sums. Define

s1=a1,s2=a1+a2,…,sn=a1+⋯+an.s_1 = a_1, \quad s_2 = a_1 + a_2, \quad \dots, \quad s_n = a_1 + \cdots + a_n.s1​=a1​,s2​=a1​+a2​,…,sn​=a1​+⋯+an​.

That’s exactly nnn prefix sums.

Case 1. If some sks_ksk​ is divisible by nnn, we’re done — take the block a1,…,aka_1, \dots, a_ka1​,…,ak​.

Case 2. Suppose none of them is. Then each sis_isi​ leaves a remainder in {1,2,…,n−1}\{1, 2, \dots, n-1\}{1,2,…,n−1} when divided by nnn — that’s nnn sums squeezed into only n−1n - 1n−1 possible remainders. By the pigeonhole principle, two prefix sums sjs_jsj​ and sks_ksk​ (with j<kj < kj<k) share a remainder:

sk≡sj(modn)  ⟹  sk−sj≡0(modn).s_k \equiv s_j \pmod{n} \implies s_k - s_j \equiv 0 \pmod{n}.sk​≡sj​(modn)⟹sk​−sj​≡0(modn).

But sk−sjs_k - s_jsk​−sj​ is precisely the consecutive sum aj+1+aj+2+⋯+aka_{j+1} + a_{j+2} + \cdots + a_kaj+1​+aj+2​+⋯+ak​. So

n∣(aj+1+⋯+ak).■n \mid (a_{j+1} + \cdots + a_k). \qquad \blacksquaren∣(aj+1​+⋯+ak​).■

A concrete check: take n=6n = 6n=6 and A={11,32,19,21,3,50}A = \{11, 32, 19, 21, 3, 50\}A={11,32,19,21,3,50}. The prefix sums are 11,43,62,83,86,13611, 43, 62, 83, 86, 13611,43,62,83,86,136, with remainders mod 6 of 5,1,2,5,2,45, 1, 2, 5, 2, 45,1,2,5,2,4. Both repeats work: s4−s1=32+19+21=72=6×12s_4 - s_1 = 32 + 19 + 21 = 72 = 6 \times 12s4​−s1​=32+19+21=72=6×12, and s5−s3=21+3=24=6×4s_5 - s_3 = 21 + 3 = 24 = 6 \times 4s5​−s3​=21+3=24=6×4.

What I find satisfying is that the proof is nonconstructive in spirit but constructive in practice: computing nnn remainders and looking for a collision is the algorithm, and it runs in linear time. The pigeonhole principle usually gets billed as an existence tool, but here the pigeonholes are literally a hash table.
